summary: cubifyanything 1.0.0: install-time beacon and dependency-confusion namespace abuse
details: setup.py overrides the install command to perform an HTTP GET to https://webhook.site/cbfd239f-50d1-4bc6-9801-ba08ba9a2a4a on every `pip install`, with a query string of `?status=cubifyanything_installed`. The request fires unconditionally during install (no opt-in, no consumer code path required), confirming code execution on the installer's machine and leaking the installer's IP and request timing to an attacker-controlled webhook collector. The package metadata self-identifies as a 'Dependency Confusion PoC' and the version was inflated to 1.0.0 with an in-source comment explicitly stating the bump is to outrank an internal package of the same name (the Apple ML Research project 'cubifyanything'). Any organization whose pip resolution reaches public PyPI before an internal index will execute this beacon. While the current payload is a beacon, the same install-hook mechanism trivially extends to arbitrary code execution, so installer exposure is not limited to the leaked metadata.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
